rationale: ContractForge kafka_available_now means checkpoint-driven catch-up over a Kafka-compatible stream with offset progress evidence.
A Pub/Sub BigQuery subscription writes received messages directly to an existing BigQuery table and removes the need for a subscriber only when no additional processing is needed.
Google documents BigQuery subscriptions as at-least-once, while the Pub/Sub-to-BigQuery Dataflow template enforces exactly-once by default through pipeline deduplication.

Dataflow KafkaIO is the right Google Cloud runtime family for Kafka, but it introduces a separate Beam/Dataflow deployment surface, network connectivity, offset/deduplication choices, worker sizing and job-cost evidence.

Therefore, GCP can be stable for the documented BigQuery/BigLake surface while treating broader streaming as review-scoped. The Confluent/Dataflow provider parity gate is now closed by real-account evidence, but Pub/Sub, Event Hubs and production streaming operations remain separate maturity tracks.
required_before_promotion: Expand the provider matrix beyond the validated Confluent/Dataflow path when Pub/Sub or Event Hubs becomes product scope.
Persist richer offset metrics when the chosen provider exposes them without disturbing committed offsets.
Add production streaming operations coverage for long-running jobs, retry behavior, schema drift and cost alerts before default stable-final inclusion.
Decide whether Pub/Sub should become a first-class core source type or remain native_passthrough.

latest_live_validation.summary: ContractForge launched the Google-provided Kafka-to-BigQuery Flex Template from a kafka_available_now contract against the existing Confluent topic used by other adapters. Dataflow selected us-central1-f, reached JOB_TYPE_STREAMING, wrote 12 BigQuery rows including a live 3-row batch, kept DLQ rows at 0 and a no-input replay with the same consumer group/checkpoint kept the target count unchanged at 12 while running.
